display.purpose: Page titles and one or two emphasis moments per route. Fraunces, an opsz-aware editorial serif. Reserved by The Display-Is-A-Deposit Rule.

body.purpose: Default for all UI elements: tables, controls, descriptions, status copy. Outfit, a clean geometric sans. 220+ occurrences in the codebase.

label.purpose: Form field labels and card titles. Uppercase Outfit at 12px with 0.08em tracking. Signals 'this is metadata, the content below is the value'.

shadows.name: modal
shadows.purpose: Modal dialogs over a backdrop. State-only.

motion.name: color-fade
motion.purpose: Body theme transition; minimal and unobtrusive.

motion.name: interactive
motion.purpose: Default for links and buttons. Quiet feedback, not choreographed.

breakpoints.name: sm
breakpoints.purpose: Phone landscape and up; switches form buttons from full-width to content-width.

breakpoints.name: xl
breakpoints.purpose: Wide desktop; data tables show optional columns.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The single primary action on a view. Brass Tally on Ledger Navy — the only place the accent earns full saturation.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #D4A64A; color: #0B1F3A; padding: 8px 16px; border: none; border-radius: 8px; font-family: 'Outfit', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; cursor: pointer; transition: all 0.15s ease; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #B88A2E; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #D4A64A; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { opacity: 0.5; cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: The cancel or escape pair to the primary. Bordered, transparent, restrained.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { background: transparent; color: #E6EAF0; padding: 8px 16px; border: 1px solid #1a3560; border-radius: 8px; font-family: 'Outfit', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 0.15s ease; } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { background: #122a4a; } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #D4A64A; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Input
components.description: Form field inset into a card. The brass focus ring doubles as the AA-required focus indicator.
components.css: .ds-input { width: 100%; background: #122a4a; color: #E6EAF0; padding: 8px 12px; border: 1px solid #1a3560; border-radius: 8px; font-family: 'Outfit', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; transition: border-color 0.15s ease, box-shadow 0.15s ease; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #5a6a82; } .ds-input:focus { outline: none; border-color: #D4A64A; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px rgba(212, 166, 74, 0.3); } .ds-label { display: block; margin-bottom: 6px; color: #5a6a82; font-family: 'Outfit', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 600; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.08em; }

components.name: Card
components.description: The default container. Flat at rest, bordered, no shadow. Surface tone alone provides hierarchy.
components.html:
May Forecast
€9,931.04
Projected end of month
components.css: .ds-card { background: #0B1F3A; color: #E6EAF0; border: 1px solid #1a3560; border-radius: 12px; overflow: hidden; } .ds-card-header { padding: 16px 24px; border-bottom: 1px solid #1a3560; } .ds-card-title { color: #5a6a82; font-family: 'Outfit', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 500; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.05em; } .ds-card-body { padding: 16px 24px; } .ds-amount { font-family: 'Outfit', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 2rem; font-weight: 500; color: #E6EAF0; line-height: 1.1; } .ds-meta { margin-top: 4px; color: #9ba8bd; font-family: 'Outfit', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.75rem; }

components.name: Sidebar Item (Active)
components.description: Active navigation row. Brass tint background and brass label, brass icon via currentColor inheritance.
components.css: .ds-nav-item { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 12px; padding: 8px 12px; border-radius: 8px; color: #7a8da6; font-family: 'Outfit', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; text-decoration: none; transition: all 0.15s ease; } .ds-nav-item:hover { background: rgba(212, 166, 74, 0.06); color: #E6EAF0; } .ds-nav-item--active { background: rgba(212, 166, 74, 0.12); color: #D4A64A; } .ds-nav-icon { width: 18px; height: 18px; }

components.name: Status Badge (Settled)
components.description: Status indicator paired with a label. Color alone never conveys state.
components.html: Settled
components.css: .ds-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 4px; padding: 4px 8px; border-radius: 9999px; font-family: 'Outfit', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1; } .ds-badge--success { background: rgba(74, 222, 128, 0.12); color: #4ade80; } .ds-badge-icon { width: 12px; height: 12px; flex-shrink: 0; }

narrative.overview:
A premium financial ledger reimagined for the screen. The system descends from the user's monthly Google Sheet — line-item budgets, per-account separation, executed-vs-forecast totals — but rejects every spreadsheet visual cue (gridlines, uniform rows, no hierarchy). The aesthetic is editorial: a serif display face, a calm dark-navy field, and a single brass accent that earns its place. It is meant to feel like a kept book, not a control panel.

Two registers must coexist without friction: a solo user planning ahead at 11pm on a Tuesday, and a couple reviewing the month over a Saturday coffee. Neither should feel patronized by friendly illustration, nor cornered by corporate stiffness. Numbers, dates, and statuses are the heroes; chrome serves them.

What this system explicitly rejects: the bank-app idiom (heavy navy-and-white corporate chrome, paternalistic copy, big circular avatar greetings) and the spreadsheet skin (gridlines as hierarchy, identical rows, no weight contrast). The Better Decision is a planning tool that happens to handle money, not a transactional account viewer.
keyCharacteristics: Calm at rest, expressive on state
Dark default, light theme available and equally considered

rules.name: The One Brass Rule
rules.body: The accent appears at most twice on any screen, and ideally once. Its rarity is the point.

rules.name: The Sidebar-Always-Navy Rule
rules.body: The product chrome stays the brand's deepest navy in both themes. Light theme adapts the data canvas, not the navigation frame.

rules.name: The No Off-Token Rule
rules.body: Every color used in the app must come from the theme tokens in globals.css. Raw Tailwind palette colors are forbidden.

rules.name: The Display-Is-A-Deposit Rule
rules.body: Fraunces is reserved for page titles and one or two emphasis moments per route. A deposit you can spend once or twice; spending it three times over-counts.

rules.name: The Number Voice Rule
rules.body: Currency, dates, and decimal amounts always use Body (Outfit), never Display. Numbers in a serif read as branding; in a clean sans they read as data.

rules.name: The Body-Is-Sm Rule
rules.body: The default body size is text-sm (14px), not text-base. Tables and dense data benefit from the tighter scale.

rules.name: The Tonal Depth Rule
rules.body: Depth comes from surface / surface-raised / surface-overlay. Reach for a darker or lighter surface variant before reaching for a shadow.

rules.name: The State-Only Shadow Rule
rules.body: Shadows mark transient state, not permanent hierarchy. A card with shadow at rest is wrong.

rules.name: The Composed-Utility Rule
rules.body: Component primitives are exported strings in lib/styles.ts, not React components. New primitives go there; ad-hoc Tailwind that duplicates an existing primitive is wrong.

rules.name: The Pressable-Surfaces Rule
rules.body: Anything pressable has a visible focus state using Brass Tally. Default browser focus rings are forbidden.

dos: Do use theme tokens (bg-surface, text-text-primary, border-border) for every color; raw Tailwind palette utilities are forbidden.
Do lead with Body (Outfit) for any UI element that displays a number, date, or status.
Do reserve Brass Tally for primary CTAs, focus states, and the active sidebar item.
Do prefer surface-tone changes over shadows for elevation at rest.
Do import primitives from lib/styles.ts (btnPrimary, btnSecondary, card, input, label).
Do include min-h-[44px] on any pressable affordance whose parent is touch-likely.
Do keep the sidebar navy in both themes.
Do pair color-coded status with a label, icon, or shape — never color alone.
donts: Don't look like a bank app. No heavy navy-and-white corporate chrome on the data surfaces, no paternalistic copy, no big circular avatar greeting.
Don't look like a spreadsheet skin. Hierarchy comes from typography, color, spacing, and grouping — not gridlines and uniform rows.
Don't use border-left or border-right greater than 1px as a colored accent stripe.
Don't wrap text in a gradient. Use a single solid color. Emphasis through weight or scale.
Don't put box-shadow on a card at rest. Shadows are state, not decoration.
Don't use Fraunces for numbers, currency, or status text.
Don't introduce a new accent. The system has one.
Don't use raw Tailwind palette colors (amber-500, slate-700, gray-200).
Don't rely on color alone to convey state.
Don't put a shadow on the sidebar to separate it from the content.
